beats.narration: On a production part, cracks come in every orientation. The classic answer: two complete inspections. Multidirectional equipment collapses them into one.

payload.title: MULTIDIRECTIONAL
payload.sub: Every flaw orientation in one operation.

beats.narration: Inside, independent magnetizing circuits share the part: a head shot drives the circular field, a built-in coil the longitudinal, alternating many times each second.

payload.heading: Two circuits, one part
payload.note: CIRCULAR ↔ LONGITUDINAL · MANY TIMES A SECOND

beats.narration: One hard rule: the part keeps only the last direction fired. Particles must be there, mobile, while the fields alternate: continuous method only.

payload.heading: Continuous method only
payload.note: RESIDUAL WOULD KEEP ONLY THE LAST DIRECTION

beats.narration: Second demand: balance. Ammeters alone can't prove it. Artificial flaw shims, in two or more orientations at several spots, must show every notch crisply.
One operation, every orientation: keep it live and prove it. Bath flowing while the fields alternate; balance shown on shims.

payload.sub: Live bath during the shots — balance proven on shims.
